title: Pack a boolean flag in bit 0 and a uint255 value in bits 1..255 of one storage slot
description: When a boolean and a near-uint256 value need to share a single slot, pack them as: slot = (value << 1) | flag. Reading: flag = slot & 1, value = slot >> 1. Writing the flag without disturbing the value: sstore(slot, or(1, sload(slot))). This pattern is used in Solady Timelock for the (done, readyTimestamp) storage layout — readyTimestamp uses 255 bits (bounded to 2^254-1) so done can occupy bit 0.
when_to_apply: Storage layouts pairing a boolean state with a value bounded below 2^255 (timestamps, indices, role bitmaps)
when_not_to_apply: When the value may exceed 2^254-1, or when value and flag are updated separately at high frequency (each write rewrites the full slot)
